name: Fleet Device Management API
what_it_does: Open-source device management and endpoint security platform built on osquery with REST API. Provides SQL-queryable endpoint visibility, MDM capabilities, vulnerability management, and compliance monitoring across macOS, Windows, and Linux.
use_cases: Running live and scheduled osquery SQL queries across device fleets via REST API
Querying device vulnerability exposure and patch status for remediation agents
Managing macOS MDM profiles, OS updates, and software installs programmatically
Enforcing CIS benchmarks and compliance policies across heterogeneous OS fleets
Building CMDB integrations with real-time device inventory and health data
not_for: Organizations without technical operations teams to self-host Fleet
Pure Windows shops — Fleet's strengths are cross-platform with macOS MDM
best_when: An agent needs structured, SQL-queryable endpoint data with MDM control capabilities across mixed macOS/Linux/Windows environments in a security-focused organization.
avoid_when: You need a fully managed MDM SaaS without self-hosting complexity — use Kandji or Jamf.

auth.notes: API tokens with role-based access (Admin, Maintainer, Observer, GitOps). Per-team token scoping for multi-tenant environments. Token-based auth for all REST API calls. Fleet GitOps for declarative config management.

pricing.paid_tiers: Fleet Premium from $7/device/month adds MDM, vulnerability management, and support
pricing.notes: Fleet open-source is fully functional for osquery management. Fleet Premium adds MDM, vulnerability management, CIS benchmarks, and enterprise support. Strong open-source community.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted; configurable via Fleet server settings

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Well-documented REST API with examples. Live query and scheduled query APIs are clearly documented. Error responses are JSON with descriptive messages. Fleet's GitOps mode enables declarative configuration.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Configuration operations are idempotent via GitOps mode. Query operations are read-only SQL — inherently idempotent. Policies and packs can be applied declaratively.
known_agent_gotchas: Live queries are async — result polling required; devices must be online
MDM features require Apple APNs certificates — additional setup beyond basic osquery
Team-based multi-tenancy requires proper token scoping — cross-team data leakage risk if misconfigured
fleetd agent (replaces osqueryctl) required for full MDM and software management features

security_score_components.security_notes: MIT open-source — full code auditability. Role-based API tokens with team scoping. osquery provides read-only endpoint visibility. TLS enforced for server communication. GitOps mode reduces credential exposure.
